title: Lemons, Passionfruit and Avocado! Oh My!
summary:
Our first impression of our Harvest Host home for the night was that the driveways were quite steep! We tentatively drove up to a barn, not sure what we would find, and were greeted by a friendly woman, Yolanda, and three barking dogs. We hopped in her 4x4 and she showed us where we could sleep for the night but it was up a very steep, dirt road that both Colin and I were too scared to drive so she directred us to a lower pullout right alongside endless rows of passionfruit vines. We had a quiet evening listenting to the coyotes and frogs from the nearby pond. As we were headed out in the morning, Yolanda loaded us up with a bag full of passionfruit, lemons, macadamia nuts and a bottle of fresh squeezed lemon juice. We also got to pick our own avocaods from their trees, and they were some of the biggest avocaods I have ever seen. We chatted a bit with her and her husband, exchaning stories of Montana, before we hit the road again.

-Helen

photos.caption: The view from our camp spot at the orchards
Our haul of goodies that we bought from the orchard, including lemons, avocados, passionfruit and macadamia nuts.
